rulings.Case Title: NETSANET ABRAHA VS. SYLVIA SHAFTO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order For Joinder Of Uninsured Motorist Claim With Third-Party Civil Action For Purposes Of Trial
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, January 27, 2010, line 1, RESPONDENT FARMERS INSURANCE EXCHANGE Petition For Order For Joinder Of Uninsured Motorist Claim With Third-Party Civil Action For Purposes Of Trial. Off Calendar, per moving party. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: ASIAN, INC et al VS. THE HARTFORD STEAM BOILER INSPECTION AND INSURANCE et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, January 27, 2010, line 2, DEFENDANT THE HARTFORD STEAM BOILER INSPECTION AND INSURANCE MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Motion denied, failure to shift burden of proof. Insufficient evidence presented to conclusively show that Asian sustained no loss. Defendant presents no affirmative evidence showing that plaintiff cannot prove its claim. CCP section 437c(p). =(302/CWW)
Set for hearing on Wednesday, January 27, 2010, line 3, DEFENDANT SUSAN SALISBURY MOTION TO TRANSFER. Motion to transfer denied. Both requests for sanctions denied. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: STANDARD PACIFIC CAPITAL HOLDINGS LLP VS. STANDARD PACIFIC PARTNERS, L.P. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Standard Pacific Capital Holdings, Lllp'S Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Pending Litigation Unrelated To Provisional Remedies (Transferred From Dept. 301, Per Recusal Of Judge Busch)
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, January 27, 2010, line 11, Plaintiff Standard Pacific Capital Holdings, LLP'S Motion To Compel Arbitration and Stay Pending Litigation Unrelated To Provisional Remedies (Transferred From Dept. 301, Per Recusal Of Judge Busch). Motion granted, entire action stayed. parties may seek provisional remedies pursuant to CCP section 1281.8 if appropriate. =(302/CWW)

Off Calendar, as moot. Defendant posted bond for $11,670.00 in compliance with Labor section 98.2. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: SERVICE EMPLOYEES INTERNATIONAL UNION, LOCAL UNION
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petitioner Service Employees International Union, Local Union'S Petition To Compel Arbitration; Request For Judicial Notice
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, January 27, 2010, line 7, Petitioner Service Employees International Union, Local Union's Petition To Compel Arbitration. Petition denied. A severe budget shortfall resulted in the layoffs of the subject employees. The layoffs are an exclusive management decision which does not properly come before an arbitrator. (See Engineers & Architects Assn. vs. Community Development Dept. (1994) 30 Cal.App. 4th 644.)=(302/CWW)

Set for hearing on Wednesday, January 27, 2010, line 8, DEFENDANT RICK FISCHER DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Is sustained, with leave to amend to plead facts in complaint regarding date of service of notice to quit. No opposition filed. =(302/CWW)
